name: PR-CR 82 Las Raíces Reservoir - Las Parras Pool - Airfield Trail
name_original: Sendero Pantano - Charco Las Parras - Campo de Aviación - Puente romano (PR-CR 82) — circular desde Saceruela
description: Hike the 7.5-mile Sendero Pantano circular trail from Saceruela, exploring a reservoir, historical airfield, and Roman bridge.
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around Montes Sur offer a variety of landscapes, from gentle paths through forests and valleys to routes featuring natural water features. The region is characterized by diverse topography, providing accessible options for various skill levels. Many areas feature well-maintained paths, making them suitable for beginner hiking routes Montes Sur. Best easy hiking trails around Montes Sur The most popular easy hiking route is Ascent to Cerro de San Blas — round trip from Baterno, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route features an elevation gain of 577 feet (176 meters). Another top favourite among local hikers is Santa Eufemia – Miramontes Castle loop from Santa Eufemia, an easy 3.2 miles (5.2 km) path. This trail includes an elevation gain of 765 feet (233 meters) and offers varied scenery. Local hikers also love the Chillón Castle loop from Chillón, a 2.8 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through local terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Hiking around Montes Sur is defined by serene forest paths, fertile valleys, and accessible trails. The network offers options for different ability levels, with 5 easy hiking trails available. The routes in Montes Sur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Montes Sur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in Montes Sur?
A: Montes Sur offers 5 easy hiking trails, providing accessible options for various skill levels. In total, there are 20 hiking routes across all difficulty levels in the region.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in Montes Sur?
A: Yes, Montes Sur features several circular easy walks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1268742662/ascent-to-cerro-de-san-blas-round-trip-from-baterno" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Cerro de San Blas — round trip from Baterno&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chillón Castle loop from Chillón&lt;/a&gt; is another great option at 4.4 km.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on easy hikes in Montes Sur?
A: Easy hikes in Montes Sur typically lead through serene forest paths and fertile valleys. The region's diverse topography ensures varied scenery, even on gentler routes, often featuring picturesque landscapes and natural elements.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions along the easy hiking trails?
A: Yes, some easy trails in Montes Sur offer access to interesting historical and cultural sites. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Eufemia – Miramontes Castle loop from Santa Eufemia&lt;/a&gt; provides varied scenery that may include historical points. You can also explore attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Mining Park&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5620080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Bullring&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable points of interest in the wider Montes Sur area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in Montes Sur?
A: The easy trails in Montes Sur are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers often praise the accessible paths and varied terrain, making them enjoyable for a range of abilities.

Q: Are there any easy hikes that pass through towns or villages?
A: Yes, some easy routes offer a blend of nature and local life. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Municipal Market loop from Almadén&lt;/a&gt; is a short 3.18 km trail that starts and ends in Almadén, allowing you to experience the local surroundings.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of easy hikes in Montes Sur?
A: Easy hikes in Montes Sur generally range from 3 to 5 kilometers in length. Most can be completed within 1 to 1.5 hours, making them suitable for shorter outings. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt; is about 4.95 km and takes around 1 hour 19 minutes.

Q: Are there any easy trails with minimal elevation gain?
A: Yes, Montes Sur offers easy trails with relatively minimal elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, has an elevation gain of only 48 meters, making it a very gentle option.

Q: Can I find easy trails that are part of a longer route or historical path?
A: Yes, some easy trails in Montes Sur are part of or connect to historical paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1197260438/miners-trail-stage-1" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Trail - Stage 1&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that hints at the region's historical context, offering a glimpse into its past.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest near the easy hiking areas?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, Montes Sur is home to several significant landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miners' Museum&lt;/a&gt; to learn about the region's industrial heritage, or see the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5236773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of the Star&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a highlight.

Q: What makes Montes Sur a good region for easy hiking?
A: Montes Sur is excellent for easy hiking due to its combination of gentle, accessible paths, diverse topography, and serene natural environments like forests and valleys. The region provides well-maintained trails suitable for various skill levels, ensuring an enjoyable experience for beginners and those seeking a relaxed walk.
tips: Saceruela is a small rural town next to the CM-415 road. Rest in the Nuestra Señora de las Cruces Parish located in the Plaza de la Constitución. The town is one of the starting points of the historic route of the Camino de Guadalupe de Levante that connects the Iberian Peninsula with the town of Guadalupe in Cáceres.
Swamp located near Saceruela, Ciudad Real. From the CM-4110 highway we arrive along a dirt road, approx. 1 km.
An airfield used by Republican aviation during the civil war, built in 1937. You can visit the bunker, in perfect condition.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike Sendero Pantano - Charco Las Parras - Campo de Aviación - Puente romano (PR-CR 82) — circular desde Saceruela?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 12-13 kilometers long and typically takes around 3 hours to complete on foot. This estimate is for hiking and does not include extended stops at points of interest.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as moderate, while the Saceruela town hall website describes it as having low difficulty. This suggests it's generally accessible to most hikers, though it does offer varied terrain. It's a good option for those looking for a slightly longer, engaging walk without extreme challenges.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The circular route starts and ends in Saceruela. You can typically find parking within the town, as the trail concludes at Plaza El Pilar. Specific trailhead parking information is not detailed, but starting from the town center is common for circular routes like this.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a diverse experience, passing by natural areas and recreational spots along the Esteras river and the Las Raíces Reservoir. Key points of interest include the Presa Las Raíces (dam), Charco de las Parras (a natural pool), the historical Airfield, and a Roman Bridge. The route also goes through olive groves and follows the Cañada Real de las Merinas, a traditional livestock trail.

faqs.question: Is Sendero Pantano - Charco Las Parras - Campo de Aviación - Puente romano (PR-CR 82) — circular desde Saceruela dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs are not provided, trails in this region are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to carry waste bags to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike in this region, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be very hot, so if hiking then, it's advisable to start early in the morning. Winters are usually mild, but check local weather conditions for rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike this public trail. It is freely accessible to all.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, encompassing natural areas, recreational spots, olive groves, and sections of a traditional livestock trail (Cañada Real de las Merinas). Expect a mix of dirt paths, possibly some rocky sections, and paths alongside water bodies like the Esteras river and the Las Raíces reservoir.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route largely follows the official trail named Sendero Pantano-Charco Las Parras. Campo de Aviación (covering 99% of the route). It also briefly intersects with a small section of the Camino del Levante - Etapa 1 (7% of the route).

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Saceruela, you will find amenities such as cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation options within the town, particularly around Plaza El Pilar where the route concludes.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, it's recommended to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate footwear (hiking shoes or sturdy trainers), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a basic first-aid kit. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are also advisable.
